cards.back: A specialization where only some of the template parameters are specialized, while others remain generic.

cards.front: Can functions use partial template specialization?
cards.back: No. Only classes can use partial template specialization. Functions can only be fully specialized.

cards.text:
To partially specialize StaticArray<T, size> for size 12:
{{c1::template <typename T>}}
{{c2::class StaticArray<T, 12> { ... };}}

cards.question: What types match this partial specialization?
cards.answer: Any pointer type: int*, double*, char*, MyClass*, etc.
cards.explanation: This partial specialization matches when T is any type and the template argument is a pointer to that type.

cards.concept: Common uses for partial template specialization
cards.explanation:
Partial specialization is useful for:
  • Specializing for pointer types (T*)
  • Specializing for const types (const T)
  • Specializing for specific non-type parameter values
  • Specializing template classes with multiple parameters
examples: template<typename T> class Array<T*> { };  // All pointers

cards.text: Partial specializations {{c1::ARE}} implicitly inline (unlike full specializations which are {{c2::NOT}}).

cards.front: What happens if multiple partial specializations match a template instantiation?
cards.back: The compiler chooses the most specialized one. If multiple are equally specialized, it's ambiguous and causes a compilation error.

cards.question: For StaticArray<int, 12>, which specialization is used?
cards.answer: Ambiguous - compilation error! Both partial specializations match equally.
cards.explanation: StaticArray<T, 12> and StaticArray<int, size> are both equally specialized for int and 12. The compiler cannot choose.

cards.text: In template <typename T> class Array<T*>, the {{c1::template <typename T>}} part contains the remaining generic parameters, and {{c2::Array<T*>}} shows which parameters are specialized.

cards.front: Why might you create a partial specialization for pointer types (T*)?
cards.back:
To provide special behavior for pointers, such as:
  • Deep copying pointed-to values
  • Automatic memory management
  • NULL/nullptr checking
  • Different semantics for pointer operations

cards.front: Can you partially specialize by adding const to a type parameter?
cards.back: Yes. You can create partial specializations like template<typename T> class Array<const T> for const types.

cards.text: Partial template specialization is {{c1::only available for classes}}, not for {{c2::functions}}.
Template<typename T> class Array<T*> is a partial specialization that matches {{c1::all pointer types}}.
